title: Ethylene Market Projected to Reach USD 332.61 Billion by 2035 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global ethylene market, valued at USD 187.35 billion in 2025, is expected to grow at a CAGR of 5.99% to reach USD 332.61 billion by 2035. Growth is driven by increased demand from packaging, automotive, construction, and petrochemical sectors, with polyethylene dominating as the largest derivative segment at 55.41% market share. Asia-Pacific leads regional revenue with 52.12% share, while North America benefits from abundant shale gas resources.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a key player in the ethylene market benefiting from strong demand growth across packaging, automotive, and construction sectors.
Highlighted in recent developments for expanding U.S. Gulf Coast operations with advanced digital twin technology and AI-driven process controls to improve ethylene yield efficiency.
Key player positioned to benefit from the projected 5.99% CAGR growth in the global ethylene market through 2035.
Listed as a major key player in the ethylene market with exposure to growing demand across multiple end-user applications.
Listed as a key player in petrochemical ethylene production with exposure to growing global market expansion.
Listed as a key player in petrochemical ethylene production with exposure to growing global market expansion.
Key player in the ethylene market positioned to benefit from rising petrochemical demand and sustainable chemical recycling technology investments.

title: Resin Market Industry Trends and Global Forecasts 2025-2035: Expanding Use Across Various Industries, Including Aerospace, Electronics, and Medical Devices
author: Researchandmarkets.Com
description: The global resin market is projected to grow from USD 667.16 billion in 2025 to USD 1.14 trillion by 2035, with a CAGR of 5.06%. Growth is driven by advancements in resin technology and expanding applications in aerospace, electronics, and medical devices. Polyethylene currently dominates, while polyethylene terephthalate (PET) is expected to grow rapidly. Asia leads in market share, particularly China and India.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Featured among leading companies in a market projected to nearly double from $667B to $1.14T by 2035, with increasing demand for advanced resin applications.
Included in the competitive landscape of a growing resin market benefiting from technological advancements and expanding use in high-performance applications.
Listed as a key player in the resin market experiencing sustained growth driven by demand in packaging, coatings, and electronics sectors.
Featured among major resin market participants positioned to benefit from market expansion and growing applications in aerospace and electronics.
Key player in the resin market with exposure to growing segments including polyethylene and polyethylene terephthalate with strong demand projections.
Listed as a key player in the expanding resin market with strong growth projections (5.06% CAGR) across high-demand sectors including aerospace, electronics, and medical devices.

title: LyondellBasell announces quarterly dividend
author: Sarah Allen
description: LyondellBasell Industries (NYSE: LYB) announced a quarterly dividend of $0.69 per share to be paid on June 8, 2026, with an ex-dividend date of June 1, 2026.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The announcement of a quarterly dividend demonstrates the company's commitment to returning capital to shareholders and indicates financial stability and confidence in cash generation. Dividend announcements are typically viewed positively by income-focused investors.

title: Antifog Additives Market Outlook 2026–2031: Expected to Exceed USD 651 Mn by 2031, Led by Asia-Pacific Growth and Supported by Rising Vertical Farming Demand, Says Mordor Intelligence
author: Mordor Intelligence
description: The global antifog additives market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 5.06% from USD 509.55 million in 2026 to USD 651.74 million by 2031, driven by rising demand from vertical farming, recyclable mono-material packaging, and stricter regulatory standards. Asia-Pacific leads market growth, while innovations in bio-based materials and advanced delivery technologies intensify competition.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Listed as a major company in the antifog additives market, which is experiencing steady growth at 5.06% CAGR through 2031, driven by increasing demand from vertical farming and regulatory compliance requirements.
Major company in the antifog additives market, positioned to benefit from growing demand in vertical farming and recyclable packaging segments with innovation-focused competition.

title: Venture Global, Alcoa, And LyondellBasell Are Among Top 10 Large-Cap Losers Last Week (April 13-April 17): Are the Others in Your Portfolio?
author: Nabaparna Bhattacharya
description: Large-cap stocks experienced broad selling pressure during the week of April 13-17, 2026, with ten major companies declining significantly. Venture Global fell 14.09% following a JP Morgan downgrade, Alcoa dropped 10.23% on weak Q1 results and asset sale news, and LyondellBasell slumped 13.93%. Other notable decliners included Dow Inc. (11.08%), Equinor (10.86%), APA Corporation (9.8%), and CNH Industrial (7.99%), driven by factors ranging from leadership changes to tariff-related supply chain concerns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock declined 14.09% following JP Morgan analyst downgrade with price target reduction from $19 to $16, maintaining Neutral rating
Stock fell 10.23% due to worse-than-expected Q1 financial results and announcement of smelter site sale to NYDIG
Stock slumped 13.93% with no specific catalyst mentioned, indicating broad sector weakness
Stock slipped 8.22%, likely in response to Amazon's acquisition of competitor Globalstar
Stock fell 5.94% following announcement of $6.4 billion acquisition of SECURE Waste Infrastructure
Stock slumped 10.86% amid broad market selling pressure in energy sector
Stock decreased 8.56% as part of broader energy sector weakness
Stock fell 9.8% amid broad selling pressure in energy sector
Stock slipped 11.08% following leadership transition announcement with CEO Jim Fitterling becoming executive chair and COO Karen Carter taking over as CEO
Stock decreased 7.99% as manufacturing-related shares traded lower due to concerns about $166 billion tariff refund delays affecting supply-chain dependent companies
